steps.title: Set up the triangle and identify the interior angle at r and s
steps.content:
The three intersecting lines form a triangle with vertices at their three intersection points.

The angle marked 106 degrees is exterior to the triangle (on the far side of line r).

The interior angle of the triangle at the intersection of lines r and s is supplementary to it:
$$\text{Interior angle at } r\text{-}s = 180^{\circ} - 106^{\circ} = 74^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Apply the triangle angle sum
steps.content:
The three interior angles of the triangle must sum to 180 degrees. We know:
  • Angle at r-s intersection: 74 degrees
  • Angle at r-t intersection: 23 degrees (marked in the diagram)
  • Angle at s-t intersection: unknown
$$74^{\circ} + 23^{\circ} + (\text{angle at } s\text{-}t) = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\text{Angle at } s\text{-}t = 180^{\circ} - 74^{\circ} - 23^{\circ} = 83^{\circ}$$

steps.title: Find x using supplementary angles
steps.content:
The angle x° is on the opposite side of line s from the interior angle we just found. These two angles form a linear pair (they share a line and are supplementary):
$$x^{\circ} = 180^{\circ} - 83^{\circ} = 97^{\circ}$$
The answer is 97.
